verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase canonically resident in the trans-Golgi where it transfers galactose to glycoprotein/glycolipid substrates. However, it has a well-documented dual-localization surface pool on the plasma membrane of multiple cell types (sperm, lymphocytes, neurons, tumor cells), where it acts as a cell-surface lectin/adhesion receptor recognizing GlcNAc-terminated glycans — most notably on sperm during egg binding and on cancer cells. The TM precursor faces the extracellular leaflet at the PM, making the catalytic domain accessible from outside.
